blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: My brother had a seizure when I was nine. Nobody could explain why. I decided then that if nobody could explain it, I would.
She said it clean. No performance. Just a fact she'd made into a direction.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's still more than most people give on a first day.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's okay. Some people find it later.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Neither did I at first.
Jonathan appeared from around the corner like he'd been timing it.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Don't.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm just saying.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You haven't said anything.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's what I'm saying.
He was looking at it the way he looks at film — reading it, not just seeing it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're going to be in there.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I wasn't trying to hype you up. I meant it like a fact.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know. That's why it hit different.
He stood there a second longer. Then he closed his locker and turned back into whoever he was in public.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye — you eat lunch yet?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: It's third period.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know. I'm already thinking about it.
That's when I saw him. The other end of the hallway. Big. Walking slow. The kind of slow that meant he owned the speed.
I didn't know his name yet. But I knew what that walk meant.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye — you heard me? Lunch. You coming or—

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. Yeah I'll find you.
